plainEnglish: If a feature can be triggered by moving the device (shake to undo) or by user motion (tilt), also provide a regular UI control for it, and let users disable motion triggering. Otherwise people who can't move the device—or who move it involuntarily—are excluded or trigger things by accident.
whyItMatters: Users with motor or mobility disabilities who can't shake or tilt a device—or who move it involuntarily—need a UI-control alternative and a way to disable motion triggers.
quickCheck: For any shake/tilt/motion feature, confirm there's an on-screen control that does the same thing and a setting to disable motion actuation.
commonMistakes: 'Shake to undo' or tilt-to-scroll with no equivalent on-screen button.
No setting to turn off motion-based actuation, causing accidental triggers.
Relying on device motion as the only way to perform an action.

codeExamples.label: Motion shortcut plus an on-screen button
Action only available via device motion
codeExamples.code:
{/* Undo only possible by physically shaking the device */}
window.addEventListener("devicemotion", maybeUndoOnShake);
